results.why: These swaps tighten a casual [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] build by improving fixing, upgrading broad utility, and replacing cards that were off-plan or outside the deck's color identity. The tri-lands smooth four-color mana, while the new spells give the deck cleaner interaction and scalable card advantage. Overall, the pass makes the list more consistent without pushing it beyond a moderate-budget casual build.

added.adarkar wastes: [[Adarkar Wastes]] gives untapped two-color fixing at a low opportunity cost.
added.an offer you can't refuse: [[An Offer You Can't Refuse]] adds very cheap stack interaction to protect your board or stop fast plays.
added.assassin's trophy: [[Assassin's Trophy]] is efficient universal removal that fits the lower curve.
added.brushland: [[Brushland]] improves early fixing by entering untapped.
added.caves of koilos: [[Caves of Koilos]] provides fast color fixing without raising the curve.
added.contentious plan: [[Contentious Plan]] is a cheap proliferate spell that keeps cards flowing.
added.dovin's veto: [[Dovin's Veto]] gives reliable low-cost interaction against noncreature spells.
added.drown in ichor: [[Drown in Ichor]] offers removal plus proliferate in one efficient slot.
added.experimental augury: [[Experimental Augury]] is a low-cost instant that digs and proliferates.
added.fellwar stone: [[Fellwar Stone]] accelerates early turns more cheaply than slower mana rocks.
added.ichormoon gauntlet: [[Ichormoon Gauntlet]] supports planeswalkers while adding repeatable proliferate value.
added.indatha triome: [[Indatha Triome]] strengthens three-color fixing and can be cycled when drawn late.
added.llanowar wastes: [[Llanowar Wastes]] improves untapped fixing for early development.
added.painful truths: [[Painful Truths]] is efficient card draw for a multicolor deck at three mana.
added.raffine's tower: [[Raffine's Tower]] improves color access and has cycling utility.
added.serum visions: [[Serum Visions]] gives cheap card selection to smooth early draws.
added.spara's headquarters: [[Spara's Headquarters]] adds strong Bant fixing with late-game cycling.
added.swan song: [[Swan Song]] is a one-mana answer that helps protect key permanents and turns.
added.talisman of curiosity: [[Talisman of Curiosity]] ramps efficiently while fixing two important colors.
added.talisman of progress: [[Talisman of Progress]] is a cheap rock that improves early consistency.
added.talisman of resilience: [[Talisman of Resilience]] adds low-cost ramp and color fixing.
added.tezzeret's gambit: [[Tezzeret's Gambit]] combines card draw with proliferate at a reasonable cost.
added.underground river: [[Underground River]] gives untapped fixing to support earlier interaction.
added.yavimaya coast: [[Yavimaya Coast]] improves early color access without slowing development.
added.zagoth triome: [[Zagoth Triome]] expands three-color fixing and cycles away when unnecessary.

results.why: This pass increases the deck's ability to answer threats at instant speed and across permanent types, which fits the add_interaction goal for [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]]. Cuts mostly come from excess mana sources, off-plan haymakers, and slower card-draw pieces to make room for cleaner interaction.

raw.plan: This pass lowers the deck’s intensity by replacing several high-impact finishers and oversized draw spells with steadier interaction, proliferate support, and smoother four-color fixing. [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] benefits from the added counterplay and proliferate pieces without leaning on more oppressive planeswalker lines. raw.why: This pass lowers the deck’s intensity by replacing several high-impact finishers and oversized draw spells with steadier interaction, proliferate support, and smoother four-color fixing. [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] benefits from the added counterplay and proliferate pieces without leaning on more oppressive planeswalker lines. The land swaps also help cast spells more casually and consistently in a four-color manabase.

added.arcane denial: [[Arcane Denial]] is a simple, flexible counterspell that keeps interaction efficient without being overly punishing.
added.arcane sanctum: [[Arcane Sanctum]] improves casual four-color fixing better than another basic land.
added.beast within: [[Beast Within]] gives broad removal while leaving a fair consolation token behind.
added.contentious plan: [[Contentious Plan]] adds a small card draw effect that also proliferates for [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]].
added.dovin's veto: [[Dovin's Veto]] adds reliable protection against key noncreature spells.
added.drown in ichor: [[Drown in Ichor]] is interactive removal that also supports the deck’s proliferate theme.
added.fuel for the cause: [[Fuel for the Cause]] combines a counterspell with proliferate for on-theme utility.
added.generous gift: [[Generous Gift]] is versatile removal that answers nearly anything at a fair rate.
added.ichormoon gauntlet: [[Ichormoon Gauntlet]] gives the deck a more playful planeswalker and proliferate payoff.
added.indatha triome: [[Indatha Triome]] strengthens color fixing and helps a moderate-budget manabase.
added.merciless eviction: [[Merciless Eviction]] provides a flexible reset button that fits casual multiplayer games.
added.raffine's tower: [[Raffine's Tower]] adds strong fixing for three of the deck’s colors while cycling late.
added.sandsteppe citadel: [[Sandsteppe Citadel]] improves accessible fixing across three colors.
added.seaside citadel: [[Seaside Citadel]] helps stabilize the mana for a four-color casual list.
added.spara's headquarters: [[Spara's Headquarters]] improves Bant fixing and can cycle when drawn late.
added.tekuthal, inquiry dominus: [[Tekuthal, Inquiry Dominus]] gives the deck a clearer proliferate payoff without being a fast combo piece.
added.tezzeret's gambit: [[Tezzeret's Gambit]] is efficient card draw that also advances proliferate plans.
added.zagoth triome: [[Zagoth Triome]] adds flexible fixing and late-game cycling utility.

removed.island: Extra [[Island]] copies were trimmed to make room for more effective multicolor fixing.
removed.swamp: Extra [[Swamp]] copies were trimmed to support a stronger four-color manabase.
removed.forest: Some [[Forest]] copies were cut because tri-lands and triomes cover colors more smoothly.
removed.plains: Some [[Plains]] copies were removed to reduce basic-heavy color strain in four colors.
removed.blue sun's zenith: [[Blue Sun's Zenith]] is a large mana sink that is stronger and swingier than this casual pass wants.
removed.consecrated sphinx: [[Consecrated Sphinx]] is a very high-impact value engine that can overpower casual tables.
removed.karn liberated: [[Karn Liberated]] is a harsh standalone threat that pushes the deck away from a softer play pattern.
removed.nicol bolas, dragon-god: [[Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God]] falls outside [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]]'s color identity and had to be removed.
removed.pull from tomorrow: [[Pull from Tomorrow]] is another large draw spell that was streamlined into more modest value pieces.
removed.rogue's passage: [[Rogue's Passage]] was cut because colored mana is more important than niche evasion here.
removed.stroke of genius: [[Stroke of Genius]] is a big burst draw spell that was toned down for a more casual feel.
removed.tezzeret the seeker: [[Tezzeret the Seeker]] supports stronger artifact and combo lines than this pass is aiming for.
removed.the chain veil: [[The Chain Veil]] was removed to reduce high-powered planeswalker engine potential.

results.why: This pass tightens [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] into a more reliable four-color casual build by improving mana access and upgrading interaction efficiency. The added tri-lands and triomes make the color requirements much smoother than basic-heavy or slow fixing, while compact answers and a tutor raise consistency. It also removes the off-color [[Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God]] and trims lower-impact blue cantrips and expensive draw spells for cleaner optimization.

added.adeline, resplendent cathar: [[Adeline, Resplendent Cathar]] adds a low-cost threat that produces steady tokens early.
added.aura shards: [[Aura Shards]] turns the deck’s token output into efficient repeatable artifact and enchantment removal.
added.boros signet: [[Boros Signet]] accelerates mana at two cost to help curve into [[Ghired, Conclave Exile]] and follow-ups.

added.felidar retreat: [[Felidar Retreat]] gives repeated token production or counters without needing a high upfront investment.
added.forest: [[Forest]] improves basic land consistency and supports smoother early development.
added.mycoloth: [[Mycoloth]] converts existing tokens into a strong board-building payoff at a more manageable cost.
added.secure the wastes: [[Secure the Wastes]] is a flexible instant-speed token maker that fits multiple points on the curve.
added.selesnya eulogist: [[Selesnya Eulogist]] provides a cheap body with scalable token utility later.
added.skullclamp: [[Skullclamp]] gives efficient card draw by turning expendable tokens into fresh cards.
added.song of the worldsoul: [[Song of the Worldsoul]] rewards normal spellcasting with extra token production to keep pressure up.

added.temple of plenty: [[Temple of Plenty]] improves color fixing while its scry helps smooth draws.
added.temple of triumph: [[Temple of Triumph]] improves color fixing while its scry helps smooth draws.

removed.decree of justice: [[Decree of Justice]] is expensive for this curve-tightening pass.
removed.explosive vegetation: [[Explosive Vegetation]] ramps well but costs more than the deck now wants from its acceleration.
removed.eyes in the skies: [[Eyes in the Skies]] is a modest token spell that was outclassed by more efficient options.
removed.godsire: [[Godsire]] is a very high-cost payoff that often sits dead in hand.
removed.grove of the guardian: [[Grove of the Guardian]] is a slow utility land that asks too much setup for the payoff.
removed.growing ranks: [[Growing Ranks]] is slow value that does not impact the board quickly enough.
removed.martial coup: [[Martial Coup]] is a costly sweep effect that clashes with a lower, board-focused curve.
removed.phyrexian rebirth: [[Phyrexian Rebirth]] is another expensive reset that is less aligned with proactive token pressure.
removed.rogue's passage: [[Rogue's Passage]] provides utility but does not help cast spells smoothly in a three-color deck.
removed.selesnya guildmage: [[Selesnya Guildmage]] is mana-hungry and inefficient compared with the new lower-cost token pieces.
removed.skarrg, the rage pits: [[Skarrg, the Rage Pits]] is a niche utility land that was weaker than cleaner fixing.
removed.trostani's summoner: [[Trostani's Summoner]] is a seven-mana token maker that is too slow for the revised curve.
removed.utvara hellkite: [[Utvara Hellkite]] is a powerful finisher but far above the desired mana range here.
removed.vitu-ghazi guildmage: [[Vitu-Ghazi Guildmage]] is another mana-intensive token outlet that was trimmed for speed.

raw.plan: This pass makes [[Yuriko, the Tiger's Shadow]] more casual by trimming swingy top-end bombs, premium free interaction, and efficient tutors while adding cleaner mana, fair interaction, and more combat-driven card flow. The new cards support evasive attacks and steady value without pushing combo turns or huge reveal spikes. Overall, the deck keeps Yuriko's play pattern but lowers burstiness and smooths everyday games.
raw.why: This pass makes [[Yuriko, the Tiger's Shadow]] more casual by trimming swingy top-end bombs, premium free interaction, and efficient tutors while adding cleaner mana, fair interaction, and more combat-driven card flow. The new cards support evasive attacks and steady value without pushing combo turns or huge reveal spikes. added.deadly dispute: [[Deadly Dispute]] is a cheap sacrifice outlet that turns disposable bodies into cards and mana.
added.elas il-kor, sadistic pilgrim: [[Elas il-Kor, Sadistic Pilgrim]] gives low-cost life drain value from the creature churn this deck wants.
added.generous gift: [[Generous Gift]] is flexible removal at a cleaner rate than slower catch-all answers.
added.jadar, ghoulcaller of nephalia: [[Jadar, Ghoulcaller of Nephalia]] supplies steady sacrifice fodder for very little mana.
added.lurrus of the dream-den: [[Lurrus of the Dream-Den]] helps rebuy cheap permanents after they die or get sacrificed.
added.midnight reaper: [[Midnight Reaper]] turns creature deaths into efficient card draw on a low-cost body.
added.morbid opportunist: [[Morbid Opportunist]] adds repeatable card draw from the death-heavy game plan at three mana.
added.priest of forgotten gods: [[Priest of Forgotten Gods]] converts spare creatures into cards, mana, and pressure very efficiently.
added.skullport merchant: [[Skullport Merchant]] brings a body, treasure, and a reliable sacrifice outlet in one card.
added.stitcher's supplier: [[Stitcher's Supplier]] is a one-drop that fuels graveyard value and gives easy sacrifice material.
added.unearth: [[Unearth]] cheaply returns many important low-cost creatures to keep the engine moving.
added.village rites: [[Village Rites]] is a one-mana way to cash in a creature for immediate cards.
added.welcoming vampire: [[Welcoming Vampire]] rewards the deck for making and replaying small creatures with steady draw.

removed.swamp: Extra [[Swamp]] copies were cut to reduce land flood after lowering the curve.
removed.plains: Extra [[Plains]] copies were cut to match the deck’s reduced mana needs.
removed.butcher of malakir: [[Butcher of Malakir]] is powerful but too expensive for a tighter casual curve.
removed.castle ardenvale: [[Castle Ardenvale]] is a slower mana source and token maker than the new cheap plays.
removed.castle locthwain: [[Castle Locthwain]] was trimmed as part of lowering slower value lands and excess mana sources.
removed.divine visitation: [[Divine Visitation]] is a high-cost payoff that asks for more setup than this pass wants.
removed.emeria shepherd: [[Emeria Shepherd]] sits at the very top of the curve and is too slow here.
removed.farewell: [[Farewell]] is a strong reset but expensive and awkward for a creature-based death deck.
removed.finale of glory: [[Finale of Glory]] is mana-hungry and better suited to a bigger top-end plan.
removed.high market: [[High Market]] was cut as a lower-impact utility land once cheaper sac outlets were added.
removed.merciless eviction: [[Merciless Eviction]] is another expensive sweeper trimmed to streamline the curve.
removed.ogre slumlord: [[Ogre Slumlord]] is a slower token payoff at five mana compared with cheaper engines.
removed.painful truths: [[Painful Truths]] was replaced by draw options that better synergize with sacrificing creatures.
removed.phyrexian tower: [[Phyrexian Tower]] was removed to help correct the list after validation and trimming.
removed.read the bones: [[Read the Bones]] is solid draw, but the new options are cheaper and more synergistic.
removed.requiem angel: [[Requiem Angel]] is a six-mana payoff that slows down the deck’s early development.
removed.spectator seating: [[Spectator Seating]] was removed because it falls outside [[Teysa Karlov]]'s color identity.
removed.sram's expertise: [[Sram's Expertise]] is a slower token-maker than the added low-cost creature package.
removed.utter end: [[Utter End]] was cut for more mana-efficient interaction.

added.canopy tactician: [[Canopy Tactician]] is a simple tribal lord that supports Elf combat without spiking power too hard.
added.casualties of war: [[Casualties of War]] adds a big splashy answer that is strong but slower and more casual in pace.
added.dwynen, gilt-leaf daen: [[Dwynen, Gilt-Leaf Daen]] rewards going wide with Elves through an easy, combat-focused anthem effect.
added.elvish warmaster: [[Elvish Warmaster]] makes extra Elf tokens and offers a fair tribal mana sink.
added.heroic intervention: [[Heroic Intervention]] protects a developed board without creating a fast deterministic win.
added.leaf-crowned visionary: [[Leaf-Crowned Visionary]] provides tribal card draw and a small anthem in a creature-centric way.
added.pact of the serpent: [[Pact of the Serpent]] is a flavorful burst-draw spell that scales with your Elf board.
added.poison-tip archer: [[Poison-Tip Archer]] gives the deck a slower attrition payoff for creatures dying in combat or to removal.
added.prowess of the fair: [[Prowess of the Fair]] softens board wipes by replacing fallen Elves with tokens.
added.return upon the tide: [[Return Upon the Tide]] adds a straightforward reanimation spell that can also support the Elf plan later.
added.skemfar shadowsage: [[Skemfar Shadowsage]] is a tribal payoff that converts a wide board into fair life drain.
added.skullclamp: [[Skullclamp]] turns small creatures and tokens into steady card advantage.

removed.forest: A second [[Forest]] was cut to keep the final build at 100 cards.
removed.swamp: A second [[Swamp]] was trimmed as part of the spell-heavy casual rework.
removed.allosaurus shepherd: [[Allosaurus Shepherd]] is a premium push card that raises consistency and finisher speed too much for this pass.
removed.beast whisperer: [[Beast Whisperer]] was replaced by more modest tribal draw options for a softer power band.
removed.cavern of souls: [[Cavern of Souls]] is a high-end consistency land that is unnecessary in a more casual version.
removed.coat of arms: [[Coat of Arms]] can create abrupt lethal swings that are stronger than the desired casual finish pattern.
removed.damnation: [[Damnation]] is an efficient reset button that was swapped for less universally punishing gameplay.
removed.deadly rollick: [[Deadly Rollick]] is extremely efficient interaction that pushes the deck toward a sharper power level.
removed.deathrite shaman: [[Deathrite Shaman]] is a highly efficient staple that adds power beyond the casual goal.
removed.ezuri, claw of progress: [[Ezuri, Claw of Progress]] falls outside [[Lathril, Blade of the Elves]]'s color identity and needed to be removed.
removed.finale of devastation: [[Finale of Devastation]] is a top-end tutor-finisher that makes wins too explosive for this pass.
removed.gaea's cradle: [[Gaea's Cradle]] greatly accelerates the deck and is too strong for the intended casual environment.
removed.shared animosity: [[Shared Animosity]] falls outside [[Lathril, Blade of the Elves]]'s color identity and needed to be removed.
removed.toxic deluge: [[Toxic Deluge]] is another very efficient sweeper that was trimmed to lower overall efficiency.
removed.triumph of the hordes: [[Triumph of the Hordes]] ends games suddenly and was removed to avoid non-casual kill shots.
removed.urza's incubator: [[Urza's Incubator]] is a strong tribal cost-reducer that makes Elf swarms too explosive for the new target power.

added.mosswort bridge: [[Mosswort Bridge]] rewards the deck for going wide with a hidden free cast.

added.pact of the serpent: [[Pact of the Serpent]] is a compact tribal draw spell that can refill a depleted hand.

removed.reliquary tower: [[Reliquary Tower]] provides little fixing and is less important than smoother colored mana.
removed.shamanic revelation: [[Shamanic Revelation]] is a higher-setup draw spell than the new options.
removed.shared animosity: [[Shared Animosity]] falls outside [[Lathril, Blade of the Elves]]'s color identity.
removed.terramorphic expanse: [[Terramorphic Expanse]] was removed to reduce tapped-land tempo loss.
removed.vanquisher's banner: [[Vanquisher's Banner]] is a slower tribal payoff than the added lower-curve engines.
removed.wayfarer's bauble: [[Wayfarer's Bauble]] is slower than the deck's creature-centric ramp and curve plan.
